---
title: "Figures measurements"
author: "Anne Hids"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: pdf_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

Load packages

```{R load packages}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(scales)

figurefolder <- "/rivm/r/E121554 LEON-T/03 - uitvoering WP3/MOMENTUM2/Figures/" # Define figure folder path

```

```{R Load measurement data}
measurements_particles <- read_xlsx("/rivm/r/E121554 LEON-T/03 - uitvoering WP3/MOMENTUM2/MOMENTUM2_measurements.xlsx", sheet = "Particle Data")
measurements_mass <- read_xlsx("/rivm/r/E121554 LEON-T/03 - uitvoering WP3/MOMENTUM2/MOMENTUM2_measurements.xlsx", sheet = "Mass Data") 

cnames_mass <- colnames(measurements_mass)
cnames_particles <- colnames(measurements_particles)
intersect_names <- intersect(cnames_particles, cnames_mass)

measurements_particles <- measurements_particles |>
  select(any_of(intersect_names))

all_measurements <- rbind(measurements_mass, measurements_particles)  |>
  mutate(Scale = str_replace(Scale, "Regional", "Netherlands"),
         Scale = str_replace(Scale, "Continental", "Europe"))
```

```{R set plot parameters}
# Create a plot theme
plot_theme <-  theme(
  axis.title.x = element_text(size = 30),    
  axis.title.y = element_text(size = 30),    
  axis.text.x = element_text(size = 28, angle = 65, hjust = 1),     
  axis.text.y = element_text(size = 28),
  legend.text = element_text(size = 28),
  title = element_text(size=30),
  panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),  # White background
  panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "lightgrey", size = 1),  # Major grid lines in light grey
  panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "lightgrey", size = 1)
)
```

Figure subcomparts and number of datapoints
```{R}
measurements_per_subcompart <- all_measurements |>
  group_by(SubCompart, Scale) |>
  summarise(observations = n())

Figure = ggplot(measurements_per_subcompart, aes(x = SubCompart, y = observations, fill = Scale)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  labs(x = "Sub-Compartment", y = "Number of observations", fill = "Scale") +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank())+
  plot_theme

Figure 

ggsave(paste0(figurefolder, "Observations_per_subcompart_per_scale.png"), plot = Figure, width = 20, height = 23, dpi = 1000)

```

Figure countries and number of datapoints
```{R}
measurements_per_country <- all_measurements |>
  group_by(country) |>
  summarise(observations = n())

Figure = ggplot(measurements_per_country, aes(x = reorder(country, -observations), y = observations, fill = country)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  labs(x = "Country", y = "Number of observations") +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +  # This removes the legend
  plot_theme

Figure 

ggsave(paste0(figurefolder, "Observations_per_country.png"), plot = Figure, width = 20, height = 23, dpi = 1000)
```

Figure mass vs particle datapoints
```{R}
measurements_per_type <- all_measurements |>
  mutate(measurement_type = case_when(
         str_detect(concentration_unit, "#") ~ "Number of particles",
         TRUE ~ "Mass")) |> 
  group_by(measurement_type) |>
  summarise(observations = n())

Figure <- ggplot(measurements_per_type, 
       aes(x = reorder(measurement_type, -observations), y = observations)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", fill = "#00C0AF") +  # Set fill color directly here
  labs(x = "Measurement Unit", y = "Number of Observations") +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +  # Removes the legend
  plot_theme

Figure 

ggsave(paste0(figurefolder, "Observations_per_unit.png"), plot = Figure, width = 20, height = 23, dpi = 1000)
```
